title: Saint Gendo split
wikitext:
[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jun 28, 2010 11:43 pm Azathoth wrote: I would assume it's the "Gendou is really a nice misunderstood young man being ruthlessly manipulated and unwarrantably abused by his evil wife/son/parents/college professor/apocalypse cabal" idea.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Jun 29, 2010 9:59 am Reichu wrote: Does anyone actually believe "Saint Gendo", or is it a misconception that has resulted from the suggestion "Gendo is a human being, not just an evil asshole" being exaggerated within the minds of some who see it? Just curious. It's pretty common for any attempt at proposing a middle ground to be reinterpreted toward one pole or another because... well, the human mind is lame like that. ("If you're not with us, you're against us!")

[]:             Quote Post by sephirotic » Tue Jun 29, 2010 10:10 am Azathoth wrote: I doubt there are any people who genuinely think of Gendou as a heroic figure, but there are certainly people who figure him for a tragic antihero. Spun the right way, he does sound rather heroic - you can start all manner of shit by pointing out that Gendou's motives are basically the same as those of Simon from Gurren Lagann, for example. The question is basically whether people see Gendou as an asshole or as an evil asshole. I'd argue that NGE takes the "evil asshole" side - not in the sense of actually passing moral judgment on him, but in the sense that we are not supposed to take his side. (Certainly sympathize with him by the end, of course, but we're not rooting for him.) Rebuild appears at this stage to be doing something quite different with Gendou, and I don't know where Anno's going with it but by the end of Rebuild 2.22 Gendou is easily more sympathetic than Shinji. Not exactly evil...and maybe not even so much of an asshole anymore, either. Still not saintly, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Tue Jun 29, 2010 10:50 am NGE isn't nearly as morally ambiguous as it's made at to be--at least as far as Gendo is concerned. He's a terrible father and has no respect for human life. He's clearly not meant to be someone the audience roots for or identifies with, misaimed fandom aside. Being an 'antihero' would require Gendo to be a protagonist, which he isn't.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Jun 29, 2010 8:12 pm Lucretius wrote: NGE isn't nearly as morally ambiguous as it's made at to be--at least as far as Gendo is concerned. He's a terrible father and has no respect for human life. He's clearly not meant to be someone the audience roots for or identifies with, misaimed fandom aside. Being an 'antihero' would require Gendo to be a protagonist, which he isn't.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue Jun 29, 2010 8:30 pm Yes, he does have his own side to the story, but he's still a terrible father with no respect for human life.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Tue Jun 29, 2010 9:10 pm There must be some other show where Gendo is a three-dimensional character who sings at an orphanage when he's not plotting to destroy mankind, because in the NGE I saw, he'd just a mean dude who glowers at the camera, says cryptic things, and maims people we're supposed to like. I bet Darth Vader was nice to puppies. I mean, c'mon, Star Wars isn't just a black-and-white morality tale!

[]:             Quote Post by Synapsid » Tue Jun 29, 2010 9:47 pm Lucretius wrote: There must be some other show where Gendo is a three-dimensional character who sings at an orphanage when he's not plotting to destroy mankind, because in the NGE I saw, he'd just a mean dude who glowers at the camera, says cryptic things, and maims people we're supposed to like.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Jun 29, 2010 10:45 pm esselfortium wrote: Yes, he does have his own side to the story, but he's still a terrible father with no respect for human life.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue Jun 29, 2010 10:49 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: The thing is, Gendo as we see him through Shinji's eyes in the series is hardly all their is to Gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Tue Jun 29, 2010 10:54 pm Yeah, that poor Rei. The way he saved her from superheated Eva-00 was practically akin to assassinating Archduke Ferdinand.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue Jun 29, 2010 10:59 pm Legendary wrote: Yeah, that poor Rei. The way he saved her from superheated Eva-00 was practically akin to assassinating Archduke Ferdinand.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Jun 29, 2010 11:10 pm Any of the mods care to split this whole Gendo business into the proper thread? esselfortium wrote: Yeah, that's a good point; there's also all the shit he does to Naoko, Ritsuko, Fuyutski, Rei, and everyone else.

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Wed Jun 30, 2010 4:55 am As I said in some other thread that got torn apart and reassembled in many smaller pieces... I can sympathize with Gendo when it comes to things like his unending cosmic love of Yui, and the great lengths he goes to be reunited. Although lots and lots and lots of people get hurt in the process, there is something romantic about that single-minded dedication to be with somebody else. But if we're thinking about Gendo from the perspective of somebody writing a history textbook, we're looking at a few human rights violations. ^_~ I mean, I can sympathize with Hitler if the story is done right, but that doesn't mean I give him a pass on the whole genocide thing. I can sympathize with anyone if the author tries hard enough.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jun 30, 2010 6:11 am I don't think it's possible to say Gendo was really a nice guy, but I also don't think he's anywhere near as bad as most want to say he is. He ruthlessly and callously advances towards a goal that is generally in humanity's best interest, even if his motivation to do so is selfish. There is a difference between being willing to mistreat others in pursuit of a goal and wishing to do so for it's own sake. Seriously, what does he do that's so unforgivably bad? Second impact was something he was not in a position to stop and, influenced by Yui, he seems to have bought into it's inevitability... thus making 2I actually his attempt at a least-bad outcome. He arguably provides Shinji with a better life than he would have had with him and rationalizes keeping him at a distance for his own good I don't get the feeling he had ill will towards Naoko and suspect he did have some degree of actual feeling for Ritsuko. I don't think he ever even realized what happened to her at Seele's hands, as from his perspective she's the one who betrayed him. Seele is the group threatening Fuyutsuki, not Gendo. By giving him a job it's possible he actually saved the old man's life. He treats Rei as a science project because he knows exactly how inhuman she is... and even then he still can't help getting somewhat attached to her. He certainly didn't intend to kill Toji or actively want to see Asuka destroyed, he just wasn't unduly concerned with saving either of them when balanced against global annihilation. Jet Alone was never intended to actually melt down, and it's sabotage was justified by the need for increased funding for Nerv. Kaji brought it on himself.

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Wed Jun 30, 2010 8:07 am Evangelion seems to be plagued with the same problem as several other franchises. It is a problem I call the "Space-Nazi Syndrome", or SS. The main symptom of SS is feeling sympathy or attraction towards a being or faction in a film or book that is intended to be antagonistic force in the work. A few examples of SS include the massive popularity of the Empire and Darth Vader in Star Wars, and the love for Zeon and Char Aznable of the Gundam franchise. I believe that the cause of this phenomenon is based in the whole "bad boy" aesthetic of the villian in modern media. I suppose "Saint Gendo" is a form of SS, but it is all the more confusing as Eva doesn't have the same black and white morality as other franchises. It is harder to identify any character or force as a true antagonist as the series revolves around the personal psychological problems of its characters. Gendo is a very confusing character, neither totally bad, or good, just like everyone else in the series.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Jun 30, 2010 8:46 am Gendo, a saint? Even I wouldn't stoop to calling him that! To add what NemZ has said, remember that Gendo is utterly, totally broken. He's an emotional wreck. His perspective towards human life is warped. Considering that he's been so thoroughly messed up, it's remarkable that he made it this far. Remember that we're never shown how he lives, what his life is like post-Yui. The very nature of his life, his emotions, are all walled off by the A.T. Field of the show itself. He's kind of an adult counterpart to Rei, a person who knew what emotions were like but whose emotions have largely been washed out or nullified by life. Even when he's angry, he seems barely capable of letting his anger out. He's that messed up.

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Wed Jun 30, 2010 9:48 am Oh, I've been waiting for this since the second Rebuild.... Gendo is a worse monster than most of the Angels. He turned an innocent little girl (well, about as innocent as Rei I was) into a nearly robotic puppet. He abandoned his son for no better reason than fear. I've known plenty of fathers terrified of fatherhood, but they never flat-out dumped their kids off with a teacher. I'm fairly certain Gendo had a hand in the 2I debacle, too. And then let's get the to the series proper. It's obvious Gendo doesn't give two shits about anyone but Yui, in the worst way possible. The only reason he's remotely nice to Rei is because he needs her, and she looks like Yui. He doesn't give a damn about the other pilots at all, especially Asuka. He's the reason Naoko killed herself, and he broke Ritsuko too. Let's not forget poor crippled/dead Toji, either. (Dammit, Gendo smiled while Eva-03 was being ripped apart.) I love how people keep saying that protagonists are just as ambiguous as the villains in this series

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Wed Jun 30, 2010 9:55 am i think i'll mostly stay away from this thread because i really, really hate the guy's guts and from past experience with this topic i think trying to discuss the reasons why is very likely to make the discussion either grow into a full-blown argument (which i don't want to happen) or go nowhere. i just want to state for the record that i think the man is evil. but as it's been mentioned before, it really depends on just what each person understands as evil. gendo is a very well-rounded character; we know his motivations and they are actually very understandable, very human. he's clearly no jack the ripper, and comparatively speaking i think SEELE are much worse than gendo is. however, i do think overall his actions were morally reprehensible, and since he went through with them in full awareness of this fact and did not seem to feel any regret about what he'd done until things were past the point where he could've done anything to correct it... i do consider him evil. then again, i tend to be black-and-white about these things; i don't believe in "neutral" unless we're talking about a character that did not actively participate in the conflict. but gendo was far too involved for me to consider him neutral. i can see how other people think his motivations justify his actions, though. there are a lot of factors involved in one's image of the man as a character, for example (but not limited to) one's opinion on the topic of child abandonment, their concept of true love, issues of individual-vs-collective, and whether or not they see instrumentality as a good thing. different PoVs on these issues will render a person's image of gendo in all manners, so it's no surprise that some people see gendo as a tragic anti-hero or a victim of his circumstances. all of us seek to identify with these characters on some level, especially if we've gone through some of the things he's gone through. like jimbo-san said, everybody's the hero of their own story-- gendo was, and so are each of us in our own lives. and since our experiences vary, so will our opinions on the matter. i don't think anybody around here would go as far as to call him a saint , though. although it does sound that way sometimes.

[]:             Quote Post by Dark doom » Wed Jun 30, 2010 10:27 am I doubt Gendo had a soft side to him, or posesed any good quality's at all. He manipulated WAY to many people to get to his goal's, to many for there to be a good view on him anyway.
